replace-function.label: Replacing Text with REPLACE
replace-function.summary: How to use REPLACE to substitute text in columns.

replace-intro.title: Introduction to REPLACE
replace-intro.bodyMarkdown:
The `REPLACE` function in SQL allows you to substitute all occurrences of a substring within a string with another substring. This is useful for cleaning up or standardizing text data in your tables. The basic syntax is:

```sql
REPLACE(original_text, search_text, replace_text)
```

For example, to replace all dashes `-` with spaces in a column, you could use:

```sql
SELECT REPLACE(product_name, '-', ' ') FROM products_catalog;
```

replace-use-cases.title: Common Use Cases
replace-use-cases.bodyMarkdown:
You might use `REPLACE` to:
- Fix typos or standardize product names
- Remove unwanted characters (like extra spaces or punctuation)
- Update outdated terms in descriptions

For example, if some product names use `"&"` and others use `"and"`, you can standardize them:

```sql
SELECT REPLACE(product_name, '&', 'and') FROM products_catalog;
```

replace-multiple.title: Chaining REPLACE for Multiple Substitutions
replace-multiple.bodyMarkdown:
If you need to make several replacements, you can nest `REPLACE` functions. For example, to replace both `-` with a space and `&` with `and`:

```sql
SELECT REPLACE(REPLACE(product_name, '-', ' '), '&', 'and') FROM products_catalog;
```

Each `REPLACE` acts on the result of the previous one.

replace-syntax-single-choice.title: What does the REPLACE function do in SQL?
replace-syntax-single-choice.prompt: Choose the best description of the REPLACE function.

options.a: It removes rows from a table.
options.b: It substitutes all occurrences of a substring with another substring in a string.
options.c: It changes the data type of a column.

replace-basic-code.title: Replace Characters in Product Names
replace-basic-code.prompt: Write a SQL query to select all product names from the `products_catalog` table, replacing all dashes `-` with spaces.
replace-basic-code.starterCode:
SELECT REPLACE(product_name, '-', ?) AS cleaned_name
FROM products_catalog;

replace-multi-choice.title: When should you use REPLACE?
replace-multi-choice.prompt: Select all situations where using REPLACE is appropriate.

options.b: To remove unwanted characters from strings
options.c: To change the order of rows in a table
options.d: To update outdated terms in descriptions

replace-nested-code.title: Chaining REPLACE Functions
replace-nested-code.prompt: Write a SQL query to select all product names from `products_catalog`, replacing all dashes `-` with spaces and all `&` with `and`.
replace-nested-code.starterCode:
SELECT REPLACE(REPLACE(product_name, '-', ' '), '&', ?) AS cleaned_name
FROM products_catalog;
